zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Lewis Clifford於大約1359年出生在英國肯特波炳時，他的父親Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford27歲，母親Maud de Beauchamp25歲。他於1373年2月12日與Eleanor DeMowbray在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1404年12月5日在他的家鄉享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Lewis Clifford于大约1359年出生在英国肯特波炳时，他的父亲Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford27岁，母亲Maud de Beauchamp25岁。他于1373年2月12日与Eleanor DeMowbray在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1404年12月5日在他的家乡享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Lewis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form, since the Middle Ages, of the French name Louis . In modern use it is also in part a transferred use of the surname derived from this given name, or from Anglicized forms of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh ’ or Welsh Llewelyn .

variants.name: Lewys
meanings: form, since the Middle Ages, of the French name Louis . In modern use it is also in part a transferred use of the surname derived from this given name, or from Anglicized forms of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh ’ or Welsh Llewelyn .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Lewis Clifford was born about 1359, in Bobbing, Kent, England, his father, Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford, was 27 and his mother, Maud de Beauchamp, was 25. He married Eleanor DeMowbray on 12 February 1373, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 5 December 1404, in his hometown, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Lewis Clifford est né environ 1359, à Bobbing, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Maud de Beauchamp, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor DeMowbray le 12 février 1373, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 5 décembre 1404, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Lewis Clifford um 1359 in Bobbing, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford 27 und seine Mutter Maud de Beauchamp 25. Er hat am 12. Februar 1373 in England, United Kingdom Eleanor DeMowbray geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 5. Dezember 1404 im Alter von 46 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Lewis Clifford è nato il circa 1359 nel Bobbing, Kent, England, il suo padre, Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Maud de Beauchamp, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor DeMowbray il 12 febbraio 1373, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 5 dicembre 1404, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 46 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Lewis Clifford(이)가 대략 1359년 영국 켄트 보브빙에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Beauchamp(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1373년 2월 12일 영국 잉글랜드에서 Eleanor DeMowbray(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1404년 12월 5일 그의 고향에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Lewis Clifford nasceu aproximadamente 1359, em Bobbing, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Beauchamp, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor DeMowbray em 12 de fevereiro de 1373, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 5 de dezembro de 1404, em sua cidade natal, com 46 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Lewis Clifford родился около 1359 г. в местности боббинг, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Beauchamp, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor DeMowbray 12 февраля 1373 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 5 декабря 1404 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 46 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Lewis Clifford nació aproximadamente 1359, en Bobbing, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Maud de Beauchamp, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Eleanor DeMowbray el 12 de febrero de 1373, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 5 de diciembre de 1404, en su pueblo, a la edad de 46 años.
